steps.title: Simplify the equation
steps.content:
Start with the given equation $$2x-3=2x$$.
Subtract $2x$ from both sides: $$2x-3-2x = 2x-2x$$.
This simplifies to $$-3 = 0$$.

steps.title: Interpret the result
steps.content:
The equation $$-3 = 0$$ is a contradiction; no value of $x$ can make it true.
Therefore the original equation has zero solutions.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
